test_cases.input: 3-day shoot for a documentary about a hospital's emergency department. Access limited to specific ward areas. Night shift filming is the key sequence. Small crew, single camera.
test_cases.notes: Correctly clustered night shift filming as the logistical anchor and scheduled other sequences around it. Ethics and consent logistics for hospital filming were flagged. Day sequence ordering was efficient.

test_cases.input: 5-day international shoot across two countries for a documentary on refugee resettlement. Multiple interviews, travel days, and border crossing. Large crew including fixer.
test_cases.notes: Travel days were correctly modeled as partial shooting days rather than wasted days. Location clustering was applied across countries. Fixer logistics were included. Critical dependencies were accurately identified.

test_cases.input: 2-day shoot for a short documentary featuring a single elderly subject in a remote rural location. Weather unpredictability is the main risk. No crew — director and camera only.
test_cases.notes: Small crew scheduling was appropriately simplified. Weather contingency plan was specific to the remote location context — no equipment rental replacements nearby. Day 2 contingency planning was the strongest section.
